evals.name: standard-update-workflow
evals.prompt: Deploy the newly built HotPlex binary to the system and restart the service.
evals.expected_output: Skill should execute the complete 8-step workflow: build new binary (make build), verify binary timestamps, stop service (hotplex service stop), wait 2 seconds, replace binary (cp to ~/.local/bin/hotplex), start service (hotplex service start), verify service status (active with new PID), and check service logs for clean startup. Should handle 'Text file busy' error with longer wait.

assertions.name: build-executed
assertions.text: make build executed to compile new binary

evals.name: rollback-after-failure
evals.prompt: The new HotPlex binary has runtime errors and the service fails to start. Rollback to the previous version.
evals.expected_output: Skill should detect service failure in logs, execute rollback workflow: stop service (hotplex service stop), restore previous binary from backup (or rebuild from previous commit), restart service (hotplex service start), and verify rollback success with clean logs.

assertions.name: failure-detected
assertions.text: Service failure detected in logs or status

assertions.name: backup-restored
assertions.text: Previous binary restored from backup or rebuilt

assertions.name: rollback-verified
assertions.text: Rollback verified with clean logs and active status

evals.name: update-with-functional-verification
evals.prompt: Deploy the HotPlex update that includes security policy fixes for the /cd command. Verify the fix works after deployment.
evals.expected_output: Skill should execute standard update workflow (build → stop → wait → replace → start → verify), then perform functional verification by testing the /cd command in Feishu or by checking service logs for security policy messages.

assertions.name: functional-test
assertions.text: Functional verification performed (testing /cd command or checking logs)
